meta.description: M6A WBC substrate support record covering implemented store, outbox, migration, payload, and trace modules. Producer/consumer adoption, Run Authority grants, Custody leases, and status cutover are outside M6A scope and not claimed here.
meta.generated_by: M6A — T18 substrate API export and support manifest

owners.wbc: Workflow Boundary Contracts — owns the substrate store, outbox, migration, payload, and trace boundaries defined here.

m6_base_contract_revision.description: M6 WBC merge commit that landed the ExecutionAttemptLedger schema, boundary contracts, and payload policy into the consolidated codebase.

m6a_store_api_revision.description: SqliteAttemptLedgerStore with WAL-mode transactional append, reservation, monotonic sequence, idempotency-key uniqueness, exactly-one-terminal enforcement, durable start/terminal gates, persistence-failure diagnostics, reconciliation diagnostics, gap queries, and source cursors.

migration_version.policy: forward-fix-only; UNKNOWN/UNRECONSTRUCTABLE for legacy records; success never synthesized from logs, markers, receipts, or mutable state

runtime_vector.locking: retry-on-SQLITE_BUSY with exponential backoff (50ms base, 2s cap)
runtime_vector.encryption: pluggable EncryptionProvider with fail-closed semantics for CONFIDENTIAL/RESTRICTED payloads

substrate_modules.description: SqliteAttemptLedgerStore providing transactional attempt reservation and append APIs with monotonic sequence enforcement, idempotency-key deduplication, exactly-one-terminal-event guarantee, post-terminal rejection, durable start/terminal gates with typed incomplete/indeterminate/incoherent statuses, persistence-failure diagnostics, reconciliation diagnostics, gap queries, source cursors, and crash-reopen consistency checks. All invariants enforced inside single SQLite BEGIN IMMEDIATE transactions.

SqliteLedgerOutbox providing atomic ledger-event + outbox-record creation inside the same SQLite transaction, at-least-once retry with idempotency-key deduplication, ack state tracking, stale-pending crash recovery, retry-limit enforcement (MAX_RETRY_COUNT=10), and reconciliation queries (get_failed, get_stale_pending, get_outbox_summary). Outbox records are append-only projection state — they record delivery intent without granting dispatch authority.

SqliteLedgerMigrator with deterministic versioned migrations, SHA-256 checksum validation, crash-resume behavior (each migration in its own BEGIN IMMEDIATE transaction), forward-fix-only policy, idempotent duplicate-backfill, mixed reader/writer compatibility under WAL snapshots, and legacy record classification (BACKFILLED/CORRUPT/UNRECONSTRUCTABLE/UNKNOWN) with ClassificationEvidence and ClassificationSafetyError ensuring success is never synthesized from logs, markers, receipts, or mutable state.

SqliteLedgerPayloadStore enforcing six payload/reference boundaries before byte persistence (fail-closed): inline threshold via InlinePayloadPolicy, redaction of protected fields, tenant/workflow access cross-checks, secret-key pattern rejection, digest-only preservation rejection, and protected-class encryption with fail-closed semantics when no provider/key is configured. Also provides retention expiry enforcement, legal hold override, tombstone markers with DeletionEvidence, DurableRef generation, and pluggable EncryptionProvider with key-version awareness.

Non-authoritative machine-readable trace emission for WBC ledger operations. LedgerTrace binds contract version, store/API revision, code/config versions, adapter identity, run/attempt/tenant identity, parent attempt, and causal lineage. Content-addressed via SHA-256 over canonical JSON with digest and emitted_at_ns excluded from the pre-image. Factory functions (make_append_trace, make_query_trace, make_reconcile_trace) provide stable trace construction. Traces are rebuildable projections — they never mint authority, leases, or delivery decisions.

out_of_scope.producer_consumer_adoption: Producer/consumer adoption paths are outside M6A scope. The substrate provides the durable store, outbox, migration, payload, and trace boundaries; actual producer integration belongs to later milestones.
out_of_scope.run_authority_grants: Run Authority grants, authority facts, views, routing decisions, and execution verdicts are outside M6A scope and are not claimed by this support record.
out_of_scope.custody_leases: Custody leases (cloud/authority/orchestration/resident) are outside M6A scope and remain unchanged by this substrate work.
out_of_scope.status_cutover: Status cutover from schema-only to runtime-adopted state is outside M6A scope. The substrate is implementation-complete for store/outbox/migration/payload/trace but does not claim runtime adoption.
